Hey, what's going on everyone? Lead nurture process. So this is the actual process, the actions that you will need to take per lead. So day one, you're going to double dial. Double dial is your new single dial, meaning you call them and then you immediately call them in if they do not pick up. Why do we do that? Because the pick up rates are significantly higher when you do that. Because it's almost like you're actually trying to call someone. Immediately after that, if you do not get a hold of them along both dials, you will text them. All right? This is it. Now mind you, the moment you get this lead, if you do this, you are going to be able to schedule a very large percentage of them. So like speed is king. Speed is king. All right? After that, if you didn't get a hold of them with that double dial text, then you'll double dial again later on the day and then before you leave that day, double dial and then text again. So it's essentially three contact attempts. I'm counting double dial text as one attempt, double dial and double dial text. That is a great day one. And then guess what happens day two? The exact same thing. Day three, we decrease down to one double dial text and the same thing day four, same thing day five. All right? Now after that day 10, we do our last push SMS, last chance campaign, which is squeezing out the drops out of the lemon and just having a little bit of scarcity for the challenge that they are running. So once they're 10 days out, it's really like the last chance for them to get in. And that's when we kind of rake all of the leads into it. After that point, I would not be reaching out to those leads beyond large scale campaigns that I do later on and or email campaigns. But that is the process. All that process and you will read the rewards. That's a promise.
